title: Expand root partition after VDI resize

log.context: Expand root partition after VDI resize

All done. Here's a summary of what was done and the results:

**Before:** Root filesystem was 23G (47% used, 12G free)
**After:** Root filesystem is 97G (11% used, 83G free)

Steps completed successfully:
1. `growpart /dev/sda 3` — expanded sda3 from 23G to 98G (filling the full 100G disk, minus sda1/sda2)
2. `pvresize /dev/sda3` — LVM physical volume updated to see the new space
3. `lvextend -l +100%FREE /dev/ubuntu-vg/ubuntu-lv` — logical volume grew from 23G to 98G
4. `resize2fs` — online fil...(truncated)
